title: Bagalāmukhī Sādhana
century: Medieval (c. 9th–14th c.); Pitāmbarā Peeth recension 20th c.
description: The eighth of the Daśa Mahāvidyās, Bagalāmukhī embodies *stambhana* — the power to paralyse, silence, or immobilise. High caution — an advanced Śākta Mahāvidyā rite traditionally associated with the arrest of hostile force and of inauspicious speech. Preserved here as heritage / source material. Not instructional.
